summary: Spun out of MPI-135. Root cause of scarce-card connect failures = GraphQL↔REST GPU-id desync (picker lists GraphQL catalogue, REST createPod enum lags it). MPI-135 shipped the honest-fail half (parse schema-400, classify gpuUnsupported, stop the futile retry). THIS card = prevention: grey/hide unsupported cards in picker before Connect. Recommend probe-and-cache (option 2).

LIVE-PROBED the REST createPod enum (via POST /runpod/pods raw passthrough, user's key, EU-RO-1). DEFINITIVE: NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell + NVIDIA RTX PRO 4000 Blackwell are ENUM-REJECTED under EVERY id variant tried (full id, 'RTX PRO 4500', 'NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500'). NO id-remap fixes it — they are genuinely absent from RunPod's REST gpuTypeIds enum. CONTROLS passed the enum (got 500 'no instances available', not enum-reject): NVIDIA RTX A4500, NVIDIA RTX 4000 Ada Generation — proving the probe is real and Ada/Ampere 4000-series ARE createable. CONCLUSION: user's 'connected before' memory was almost certainly the Ampere RTX A4500/A4000 or RTX 4000 Ada (no PRO/Blackwell), which DO work. The PRO Blackwell 4500/4000 were never REST-createable. So 159 = grey-out is correct (no remap to chase). Probes left ZERO pods (enum-reject creates nothing; 500s create nothing). Recommend probe-and-cache: on a gpuUnsupported reject remember the id (localStorage) + grey it in the picker thereafter; the live POST /runpod/pods route is a ready-made probe if a proactive enum fetch is wanted later.

RESTRUCTURED grey-out -> GraphQL create fallback (user goal: connect to ANY picker card). 4-agent investigation (external GraphQL capability + codebase split/blast-radius + history/rationale) + LIVE PROOF. KEY LIVE RESULT: raw GraphQL podFindAndDeployOnDemand for 'NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell' returned a RUNNING pod (id oo8el8ng4rngcb, our cu128 image) — the card REST enum-rejects — then DELETED + getPod-404-confirmed via REST routes (REST manages a GraphQL-created pod; shared id namespace). GraphQL gpuTypeId is a free String (no enum), same id the picker has; console uses this mutation; not deprecated. DESIGN: REST stays primary (keeps CPU pods + all MPI-135 handling + getPod/status/specs/stop/delete); on the gpuUnsupported flag MPI-135 already emits, fall back to GraphQL create with params translated (gpuTypeIds[]→gpuTypeId, dataCenterIds[]→dataCenterId, env obj→[{key,value}], ports[]→comma-string, cloudType:SECURE). CPU __cpu__ NEVER falls back (GraphQL lacks computeType:CPU). No prior GraphQL→REST migration to re-break. Full plan + param table + acceptance tests in task.json. Ready to implement next session.
